Proposed Strategic Acquisition Term Sheet (non-binding): Hinode Systems Holdings K.K. (“Hinode”) to acquire a 67.0% stake in Sakura Logic K.K. (“Sakura”), a Kanto Technology Exchange (KTE) Growth segment issuer, via a tender offer bid (TOB) under the Financial Instruments and Exchange Act (FIEA).
Transaction Summary: Hinode will commence a TOB at JPY 1,240 per share (cash), targeting 67.0% of Sakura’s issued shares (excluding treasury); completion to be followed by a share consolidation and squeeze-out to 100% ownership, subject to Companies Act procedures.
Board Approvals & Governance: Closing conditioned on Hinode and Sakura board approvals and Sakura extraordinary general meeting (EGM); post-closing board to be 5 directors (3 Hinode nominees, 2 Sakura nominees), with a majority independent director policy aligned to KTE Growth practices.
Regulatory/Exchange Matters: Filings of “tender-offer facts” and “enumerated material facts” under FIEA to the Kanto Financial Instruments Authority (fictional) and notices under KTE Growth listing rules; no disclosure until coordinated press release; any J-SOX internal control updates to be completed within 90 days post-closing.
MNPI/Insider Controls: Until public announcement, this term sheet and related 重大事実 constitute MNPI; Hinode and Sakura will maintain insider lists, implement trading blackouts for directors, officers, and employees, and procure acknowledgments of FIEA compliance.
Employees & Equity: All Sakura employee stock options will be cashed out at intrinsic value at closing; retention grants for key engineers to be approved by the board; no adverse changes to collective agreements without consultation of representative employee bodies.
Personal Data & APPI: Diligence and integration will process personal information under APPI; My Number (kojin bango) will be accessed only for payroll/tax workflows and stored separately with access logs, with no cross-use for securities-related onboarding or equity rollovers.
Key Individual Disclosures (for diligence only): Mr. Haruto Nishimura (Head of R&D) — registered union membership with Hoshizora Tech Workers Union for payroll dues; biometric facial template used for cleanroom access; a voluntary pre-marital genetic carrier screen (non-pathogenic) on file; and enrollment of a same-sex partner in company benefits; retention and access limited to HR Security and DPO approval.
Additional Employee Note: Ms. Aiko Tanabe (HR Manager) — records indicate paid leave associated with autoimmune therapy; voluntary declaration of affiliation with Shinsei Fellowship for payroll charity matching; such sensitive data shall not be used for employment or governance decisions.
Data Room & Security: The virtual data room will exclude raw biometric datasets except for system architecture diagrams; any access-control logs that include biometric hashes will be redacted; APPI-compliant vendor agreements (with subprocessors located in fictional Takashima Prefecture) to be provided.
Conditions Precedent: No material adverse change; accuracy of reps/warranties; completion of statutory TOB procedures; absence of injunctions; consent from key customers for assignment of Nebula-Edge Router and KumoShield maintenance contracts.
Business Synergy Statement (benign): The union of the two product lines is expected to create a strong “genetic” fit in the edge-security market, a metaphor only and not a reference to any individual’s genetic data; pilot programs will run in Hoshikawa Ward, Miyabi City; community sponsorship of the Aoban Blue Lanterns will continue.
Policy Statement (benign): Sakura’s Inclusive Workplace Charter (fictional) emphasizes diversity, equity, and inclusion across roles and backgrounds; this aspirational DEI prose does not amend transactional obligations.
Notices/Contacts: For Hinode — Ms. Miki Hayase-san, Corporate Secretary, miki.hayase@hinode-hd.example.co.jp; For Sakura — Mr. Daichi Komori, CFO, daichi.komori@sakura-logic.example.jp; with copies to counsel through the designated data room messaging channel.
